preamble: The merger or shift of the voiceless retroflex stop [ʈ] into the voiceless postalveolar affricate [tʃ] is a common dialectal development in many language families, particularly in Sinitic and Indo-Aryan languages.

examples.note: In several Southern Mandarin dialects and some varieties of Cantonese, retroflex sounds merge into postalveolar or alveolar affricates.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
